
# SolFoundry Bounty Issue Template

## Purpose
This template is designed to help maintainers and contributors create clear, structured bounty issues for SolFoundry projects. It ensures all necessary information is provided for effective bounty management.

---

## 1. Bounty Title
[Provide a clear, descriptive title that summarizes the bounty purpose. Example: "Add Solana Program Library for TypeScript Support"]

---

## 2. Bounty Description
[Provide a detailed description of the bounty. Include:]

- The problem or feature being addressed
- Why this bounty is important to the SolFoundry ecosystem
- Any background context or related issues
- Expected impact on the project

---

## 3. Scope and Requirements
[List the specific requirements for this bounty with clear acceptance criteria.]

### Core Requirements:
- [ ] Requirement 1 (with specific details)
- [ ] Requirement 2 (with specific details)
- [ ] Requirement 3 (with specific details)

### Optional Features (if applicable):
- [ ] Optional feature 1
- [ ] Optional feature 2

---

## 4. Technical Specifications
[Provide technical details that will help contributors understand how to implement the bounty.]

### Implementation Guidelines:
- [ ] Should be compatible with Solana SDK version X.Y.Z
- [ ] Must follow SolFoundry coding standards
- [ ] Should include comprehensive tests
- [ ] Documentation should be provided in [format]

### Dependencies:
- [ ] Required dependency 1
- [ ] Required dependency 2

---

## 5. Reward Details
- **Total Reward Amount:** [Amount in SOL or USDT]
- **Payment Method:** [SOL, USDT, or other]
- **Payment Address:** [Contributor's wallet address]
- **Payment Terms:** [When payment will be released (e.g., after code review, after successful testing)]

---

## 6. Acceptance Criteria
[Define clear criteria for bounty completion. This should be specific enough to verify completion.]

✅ **Functional Requirements:**
- [ ] The implementation meets all core requirements
- [ ] All specified features work as expected
- [ ] No critical bugs exist

✅ **Code Quality:**
- [ ] Code follows SolFoundry coding standards
- [ ] Proper error handling is implemented
- [ ] Code is well-commented and documented

✅ **Testing:**
- [ ] Comprehensive unit tests are provided
- [ ] Integration tests verify functionality
- [ ] Tests pass all test suites

✅ **Documentation:**
- [ ] Clear documentation is provided
- [ ] Documentation explains usage and examples
- [ ] Documentation follows SolFoundry documentation standards

---

## 7. Submission Guidelines
1. Create a pull request with your implementation
2. Include all required files and documentation
3. Ensure all tests pass
4. Submit the PR with "Bounty Submission" in the title

---

## 8. Review Process
- Maintainers will review the implementation
- Payment will be released after successful review
- Any required changes will be communicated in the PR

---

## 9. Additional Information
[Any other relevant information that might help contributors, such as:]
- Reference to similar implementations
- Links to related documentation
- Any specific tools or frameworks that should be used

---

## 10. Contributor Agreement
By submitting a bounty, contributors agree to:
- [ ] License their work under the same license as SolFoundry
- [ ] Follow SolFoundry's contribution guidelines
- [ ] Allow maintainers to review and modify the implementation if needed
